name: WebGLCanvas
summary: DPI-aware WebGL2 canvas that resizes to its parent and runs a render callback on a requestAnimationFrame loop; use it for custom GPU graphics, shaders, and visualizations rather than 2D drawing.

props.name: create
props.description:
Create a program bound to the provided WebGL2 context and canvas. Called
ONCE when the context is acquired; its identity is ref-latched, so an inline
arrow does NOT rebuild the program on parent re-render. To force a rebuild,
change the element `key`. Return `null` to signal a build failure (routes to
`onError` / `fallback`).

props.name: contextAttributes
props.description: WebGL context attributes. Read ONCE at context creation (ref-latched).

props.name: dprMax
props.description: Clamp devicePixelRatio to avoid excessive fill-rate on hi-DPI screens.

props.name: timeScale
props.description: Global time scaling applied to dt and absolute time t.

props.name: clearColor
props.description: Optional clear color, defaults to transparent. Only sets clearColor, not clearing each frame.

props.name: asBackground
props.description: If true, wrapper is absolutely positioned and fills its parent.

props.name: onError
props.description:
Called if the WebGL2 context cannot be created (unsupported) or `create()`
returns null. The canvas otherwise dead-ends blank with no signal.

props.name: fallback
props.description: Rendered in place of the canvas when initialization fails (see `onError`).

props.name: sx
props.description: Inline styles for the wrapper (supports CSS variables via Sx).

props.name: canvasClassName
props.description: Optional className for the inner canvas element.

props.name: preset
props.description: One or many style-preset names registered via `definePreset()`

bestPractices: Prefer low DPR on animated backgrounds to reduce fill-rate.
Avoid heavy per-frame allocations in program.update().
Dispose GL resources on unmount to prevent leaks.
